title: Intense Mustardy Australian Beef Flatbread
description: A flavorful and low-sodium Australian dish featuring soured cream and chive dip, long-fermentation-dough, and intense mustardy taste, all prepared on a comal.
instructions: Combine yeast, flour, and a pinch of salt-free seasoning in a large bowl to make the long-fermentation-dough.
Gradually add olive oil and mix until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes and let it ferment for 2 hours.
Preheat the comal over medium heat.
Roll out the fermented dough into a large flatbread shape.
Cook the flatbread on the comal for 2 minutes on each side.
In a separate bowl, mix soured cream, chopped chives, and whole grain mustard to make the dip.
Season the beef strips with salt-free seasoning and cook on the comal until browned.
Assemble the flatbread by spreading the soured cream and chive dip, then topping with cooked beef strips.
